title: Sony confirms the end of physical format for PlayStation and bets on a fully digital future
summary: The company will eliminate game discs starting in 2028, will remove the PS6 unit and close the PS3 store, generating protests among players and doubts about software ownership.

title: Companies cut back on the use of AI agents due to high cost and limited performance
summary: The cost and efficiency of artificial agents are far from expected, so companies are limiting their use while the technology continues to evolve.

title: Palantir is banned by European governments and its CEO sparks controversy with his statements
summary: Spain prohibits the use of Palantir systems due to security risks, while the British NHS considers withdrawing it, and its CEO defends the company.

title: OpenAI offers the US government 5% of its capital in exchange for regulatory support
summary: The artificial intelligence company proposes an equity stake to the US sovereign wealth fund, amid negotiations for the approval of GPT-5.

title: Intel increases the price of its Core Ultra 7 and 5 processors by up to $50
summary: The company updated the recommended prices of its high-end desktop CPUs, according to official listings.

title: Microsoft prepares a feature to digitize physical Xbox games
summary: Following Sony's decision, Xbox would allow converting discs into digital licenses tied to the user, following the trend towards an ecosystem without physical format.

title: Microsoft launches an AI deployment company with $2.5 billion and prepares a Copilot system
summary: The company creates 'Frontier Company' to integrate 6,000 AI engineers into enterprise clients, while developing an operating system based on Copilot.

title: Anthropic's Fable 5 model redefines AI development and forces optimizing prompts
summary: Anthropic has drastically reduced the system instructions of Claude Code to adapt to the new Fable 5 model, which requires shorter interaction.

title: AMD will raise the price of its Radeon GPUs and an engineering sample of the RX 7900 XTX is discovered
summary: AMD partners will pay 10% more for graphics cards, while a test board of the RX 7900 XTX with only 16 GB of memory appears.

title: New dynamics in the memory market: Apple turns to Chinese chips and SK hynix invests 712 billion
summary: Facing the RAM price crisis, Apple seeks alternatives in Chinese manufacturers while SK hynix and Micron adjust their strategies in the memory sector.

title: A flaw in Apple's Hide My Email exposes users' real addresses
summary: Apple's privacy feature allows attackers to discover the real email, and the company has known about it for a year without fixing it.

title: EU confirms record fine of 4.1 billion euros to Google for abuse of dominant position
summary: The Court of Justice of the European Union upholds the penalty against Google for its Android system, with no possibility of appeal.

title: Tensions at Tesla: Semi accident, stock drop, and quarterly sales increase
summary: The electric truck suffers its first fatal accident, the stock falls despite beating expectations, and the company reports a strong increase in deliveries.

title: Anthropic negotiates with Samsung to manufacture a custom AI chip
summary: The artificial intelligence company seeks to diversify its semiconductor supply beyond Nvidia, although it says Nvidia remains important.

title: Noctua updates its roadmap with new coolers for AMD and Seasonic power supplies
summary: The company prepares a low-profile cooler for AM5 and new power supplies in collaboration with Seasonic for 2027.

title: Meta advances in hardware: brain-computer interface without surgery and subscription for smart glasses
summary: The second version of its BCI allows writing with the mind, while the Ray-Ban Stories smart glasses will require a monthly payment for premium features.

title: Nvidia funds AI startups and offers discounts on cloud revenue to boost its ecosystem
summary: The company invests in startups to reduce dependence on large cloud providers and proposes a new hardware revenue sharing model.

title: United States strengthens digital privacy: Virginia bans sale of location data and Oregon sues Kalshi
summary: State authorities take action against the exploitation of personal data and illegal gambling, marking a regulatory shift in the country.

title: Mark Zuckerberg admits slow progress of AI agents and suffers public humiliation
summary: Meta's CEO tells his employees that AI agents are not meeting expectations, while a viral incident leaves him embarrassed.

title: The electric vehicle industry reconfigures: changes at Lucid, growth of Rivian, and BYD's plans in Spain
summary: The electric car sector experiences key moves: Lucid's CFO resigns, Rivian raises forecasts, and BYD announces ultra-fast chargers in Spain.
